title_en: Taharah in Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil: Maliki Responses to Legal Questions
title_id: Thaharah dalam Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil: Respons Maliki terhadap Pertanyaan Hukum
content_id:
Pendekatan Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil terhadap thaharah (bersuci) mencerminkan formatnya yang khas sebagai komentar atas responsa hukum: alih-alih menyajikan uraian sistematis tentang hukum thaharah Maliki, karya ini terlibat dengan pertanyaan-pertanyaan spesifik yang benar-benar diajukan kepada Imam Malik dan murid-muridnya, menerangi bagaimana penalaran hukum Maliki bekerja dalam penerapan daripada abstraksi.

Salah satu fitur signifikan dari bagian-bagian thaharah Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil adalah pelestarian pertanyaan-pertanyaan yang mengungkapkan keprihatinan-keprihatinan praktis kaum Muslim di Andalusia dan Afrika Utara selama periode formatif peradaban Islam. Pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang kesucian sumur yang terkontaminasi oleh hewan yang mati, status air di pemandian umum, dan penyucian pakaian yang kotor dalam keadaan yang tidak dapat dihindari mencerminkan realitas hidup yang harus ditangani oleh hukum Islam.

Komentar Ibn Rushd al-Jadd atas respons-respons Malik menunjukkan perhatian khas mazhab Maliki terhadap prinsip kemudahan (yusr) dan penghindaran kesulitan yang tidak semestinya (masyaqqah). Dalam pertanyaan-pertanyaan thaharah, ini terwujud sebagai kecenderungan untuk menyelesaikan keraguan yang memihak kesucian ketika najisnya tidak pasti, untuk membolehkan penyucian dengan cara-cara standar tanpa memerlukan langkah-langkah luar biasa, dan untuk mengakui keabsahan penyucian meskipun dilakukan dalam kondisi sulit.

Pembahasan sumber-sumber air bersama dan publik dalam Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil mencerminkan realisme hukum Maliki. Pertanyaan-pertanyaan tentang kesucian badan-badan air besar, yaitu sungai, bak besar, laut, dijawab dengan mengacu pada prinsip bahwa volume air yang besar secara efektif membersihkan dirinya sendiri dan tidak seharusnya diperlakukan sebagai najis tanpa bukti kontaminasi yang jelas yang mempengaruhi sifat-sifat air yang dapat diindera. Pendekatan praktis ini mencegah kesulitan yang tidak perlu bagi komunitas-komunitas yang bergantung pada sumber air bersama.

Analisis Ibn Rushd al-Jadd atas respons-respons tentang pertanyaan-pertanyaan kesucian haid mengungkapkan kepekaan dan kecanggihan penalaran hukum Islam klasik dalam hal-hal yang mempengaruhi kehidupan sehari-hari perempuan. Komentarnya mengontekstualisasikan putusan-putusan spesifik Malik dalam kerangka hukum haid Maliki yang lebih luas, menjelaskan bagaimana periode minimum dan maksimum ditentukan dan bagaimana mazhab menangani realitas praktis perempuan dengan siklus yang tidak teratur.

Bagian-bagian Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil tentang thaharah juga mencakup responsa tentang persyaratan penyucian untuk objek-objek yang digunakan dalam ibadah, yaitu sajadah, pakaian yang dipakai untuk shalat, dan ruangan yang digunakan untuk shalat berjamaah. Respons-respons Malik tentang pertanyaan-pertanyaan ini mengungkapkan prinsip yang konsisten: persyaratan kesucian untuk konteks ibadah harus diambil serius, namun kemampuan praktis kaum Muslim biasa untuk memenuhi persyaratan tersebut juga harus dihormati.

Membaca bagian-bagian thaharah Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil bersama Al-Mudawwanah dan Al-Muqaddimat memberi murid pemahaman tiga dimensi tentang hukum thaharah Maliki: posisi-posisi fondasi (Al-Mudawwanah), prinsip-prinsip yang mendasari (Al-Muqaddimat), dan penerapan pada kasus-kasus nyata (Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil).
content_en:
Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil's approach to taharah (purification) reflects its distinctive format as a commentary on legal responsa: rather than presenting a systematic account of Maliki purification law, it engages with specific questions that were actually posed to Imam Malik and his students, illuminating how Maliki legal reasoning works in application rather than abstraction.

One of the significant features of Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil's taharah sections is the preservation of questions that reveal the practical concerns of Muslims in Andalusia and North Africa during the formative period of Islamic civilization. Questions about the purity of wells contaminated by dead animals, the status of water in shared bathhouses, and the purification of clothing soiled in unavoidable circumstances reflect the lived realities that Islamic law had to address.

Ibn Rushd al-Jadd's commentary on Malik's responses demonstrates the Maliki school's characteristic attention to the principle of ease (yusr) and the avoidance of undue hardship (mashaqqah). In taharah questions, this manifests as a tendency to resolve doubts in favor of purity when the impurity is uncertain, to permit purification by standard means without requiring exceptional measures, and to recognize the validity of purification even when performed under difficult conditions.

The treatment of shared and public water sources in Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil reflects Maliki legal realism. Questions about the purity of large water bodies — rivers, large cisterns, the sea — are answered with reference to the principle that large volumes of water are effectively self-purifying and should not be treated as impure absent clear evidence of contamination affecting the water's perceptible qualities. This practical approach prevented unnecessary hardship for communities that depended on shared water sources.

Ibn Rushd al-Jadd's analysis of the responses on menstrual purity questions reveals the sensitivity and sophistication of classical Islamic legal reasoning on matters affecting women's daily lives. His commentary contextualizes Malik's specific rulings within the broader framework of Maliki menstruation law, explaining how the minimum and maximum periods were determined and how the school handled the practical reality of women with irregular cycles.

The Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil sections on taharah also include responsa on the purification requirements for objects used in worship — prayer mats, clothing worn for prayer, and spaces used for congregational worship. Malik's responses on these questions reveal a consistent principle: the purity requirements for worship contexts should be taken seriously, but the practical ability of ordinary Muslims to meet those requirements should also be respected.

Reading Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil's taharah sections alongside Al-Mudawwanah and Al-Muqaddimat gives students a three-dimensional understanding of Maliki purification law: the foundational positions (Al-Mudawwanah), the underlying principles (Al-Muqaddimat), and the application to real cases (Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil).

One of the المهم features of Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil's الطهارة sections is the preservation of questions that reveal the practical concerns of المسلمون in Andalusia and North Africa خلال the formative period of Islamic civilization. Questions about the purity of wells contaminated by dead animals, the status of water in shared bathhouses, and the purification of clothing soiled in unavoidable circumstances reflect the lived realities that Islamic law had to address. Ibn Rushd al-Jadd's commentary on مالك's responses demonstrates the المالكي school's characteristic attention to the principle of اليسر (yusr) and the avoidance of undue الشدة (mashaqqah). The treatment of shared and public water sources in Al-Bayan wal-Tahsil reflects المالكي legal realism. Questions about the purity of large water bodies — rivers, large cisterns, the sea — are answered with reference to the principle that large volumes of water are effectively self-purifying and should not be treated as impure absent clear evidence of contamination affecting the water's perceptible qualities. This practical approach prevented unnecessary الشدة for communities that depended on shared water sources. Ibn Rushd al-Jadd's analysis of the responses on menstrual purity questions reveals the sensitivity and sophistication of الكلاسيكي Islamic legal reasoning on matters affecting women's daily lives. His commentary contextualizes مالك's specific rulings within the broader framework of المالكي menstruation law, explaining how the minimum and maximum periods were determined and how the school handled the practical reality of women with irregular cycles.
